channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Trail Blazers take to the court for the first time after coach Chauncey Billups' arrest
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Stunned by the arrest of head coach Chauncey Billups in a sprawling alleged poker scheme, the Portland Trail Blazers took to the court Friday night under an interim coach as they navigated an unexpectedly tumultuous start to the new season.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shohei Ohtani follows historic 2-way show with World Series homer, but Dodgers routed by Jays
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Shohei Ohtani followed his two-way show for the ages with a homer in the Dodgers' World Series opener, but he also grounded out with the bases loaded in Los Angeles' 11-4 loss to the Toronto Blue Jays on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World Series: Barger, Varsho and Kirk belts homers as Blue Jays rout Dodgers 11-4 in opener
item.description: Addison Barger hit the first pinch-hit grand slam in World Series history during a nine-run sixth inning as Toronto overwhelmed the defending champions in Game 1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Terry Rozier's arrest leaves the Miami Heat stunned, and trying to figure out what happens next
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The reality began setting in for the Miami Heat on Friday when they gathered for a morning shootaround practice and Terry Rozier wasn't with the team. And that almost certainly won't change anytime soon.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blue Jays fans boo Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani ahead of World Series Game 1
item.description: Shohei Ohtani struck out in his first World Series at-bat, and Toronto fans booed the Dodgers star, who visited the Blue Jays before signing in LA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL reminds players of league gambling policy in light of federal indictments
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NFL reminded its players Friday that they are prohibited from taking part in any form of illegal gambling and betting on league games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chelsea's US private equity owners were once ridiculed. Now opinions are changing
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Against the geopolitical backdrop of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in early 2022, a consortium led by American private equity titans took the opportunity to acquire one of soccer’s crown jewels. What followed at Chelsea was torrid and, at times, bewildering.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7 NBA games DOJ says were affected by high-stakes wagers after inside information was leaked to gamblers
item.description: Federal prosecutors allege Terry Rozier faked an injury to help friends win thousands of dollars in bets, while Damon Jones shared Los Angeles Lakers player information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue Jays manager to Shohei Ohtani: We want our hat back
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — While most of baseball is saying hats off to Shohei Ohtani, Toronto manager John Schneider wants a cap back from the two-way star. Before signing a $700 million, 10-year contract with the Los Angeles Dodgers, the two-way star met with Blue Jays officials on Dec.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NBA coach Billups, Heat's Rozier arrested in FBI gambling probe
item.description: More than 30 people were arrested in connection with a federal probe into sports gambling and rigged poker games, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee baseball coach Tony Vitello reportedly hired as next SF Giants manager
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants have hired University of Tennessee baseball head coach Tony Vitello as manager. Vitello has never worked as a coach in professional baseball.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Michael Jordan is going to be part of NBC's NBA coverage. His reason: to give back to the game
item.description: Once upon a time, Michael Jordan was the unquestioned No. 1 star player in the "NBA on NBC" universe. A generation of stars later, Jordan plans to contribute his considerable insights into NBC's return to broadcasting the league.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pacers rookie Kam Jones arrested after police chase on I-65
item.description: Indiana State Police said a trooper tried around 10 a.m. to pull over a car that was "very erratic" and driving recklessly on Interstate 65.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: Former Raider Doug Martin dies in Oakland police custody
item.description: Police said Martin became unresponsive after he was taken into custody and later died at an area hospital. He was 36 years old.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: Donato scores in OT, Blackhawks edge Ducks 2-1 in Quenneville's return to Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Ryan Donato scored from the edge of the crease at 2:58 of overtime and the Chicago Blackhawks beat Anaheim 2-1 on Sunday night in Ducks coach Joel Quenneville's first game at United Center since being banned in a sexual assault scandal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pickleball eye injuries on the rise as sport gains popularity: Study
item.description: A study published in JAMA Ophthalmology on Thursday suggests that "pickleball-related eye injuries have increased at an alarming rate over the past 4 years,"
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation
